message:
Evaluate DEFAULT expressions via SQL execution instead of ad-hoc parsing

Replace the hand-rolled parse_column_default_value() function with
evaluate_column_default_value() that wraps default text in SELECT and
executes through the SQL engine. This correctly handles expression
defaults like (1 + 2), (-42), function calls (random()), and all
other valid SQL expressions that the old literal/keyword parser missed.

Key changes:
- collect_insert_default_row() replaced by collect_insert_default_sqls()
  (captures SQL text) + evaluate_default_row_from_sqls() (evaluates per
  row), ensuring nondeterministic defaults like random() produce unique
  values per inserted row in INSERT...SELECT and trigger snapshots
- Remove trigger_update_of_columns_changed() — UPDATE OF trigger column
  change detection was unreliable and is no longer used
- Remove dead code: format_expr_as_default(), parse_column_default_value(),
  current_default_time_value(), epoch_days_to_ymd()
- select_matching_rows/count_matching_rows now accept ORDER BY and LIMIT
  for correct affected-row bookkeeping with UPDATE/DELETE LIMIT
- codegen.rs: default_value_to_expr() and emit_default_value() now use
  parse_default_expr() which runs the real SQL parser instead of ad-hoc
  parsing; HAVING And/Or codegen simplified to single opcodes
- fsqlite-parser promoted from dev to regular dependency in VDBE crate

Tests: expression defaults, nondeterministic per-row defaults, trigger
expression defaults, UPDATE/DELETE with alias + ORDER BY + LIMIT.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
